channel.title: Harman Kamboj — Writing
channel.description: Essays on Web3, AI engineering, full-stack, and shipping software.

item.title: Durable execution: how I make an AI agent survive a crash mid-run
item.description: Durable execution for AI agents lets a run resume from the last checkpoint after a crash instead of redoing unsafe work. Here is how I actually set it up.

item.title: WebGPU will not fix your slow browser game
item.description: WebGPU shipped in every browser, but it will not fix a slow browser game. The real bottlenecks are download size and the main thread, not raw GPU power.

item.title: Most multi-agent systems do not need to be multi-agent
item.description: Most multi-agent systems add coordination overhead before they add value. When one good agent wins, when multiple agents pay off, and how I decide.

item.title: SSE or WebSockets? How I actually pick for real-time features
item.description: SSE or WebSockets for real-time features: how I actually decide, from someone who shipped live price and trading screens. Direction of data beats raw power.

item.title: Idempotent tool calls: do this before you let an agent retry
item.description: When a tool call times out, an AI agent often runs it twice. How idempotent tool calls and a retry budget keep retries from corrupting real data.

item.title: Your MCP server is eating the context window
item.description: Why MCP servers quietly fill an agent's context window with bloated tool schemas and oversized results, and how I keep an agent's context lean and useful.

item.title: Context rot: why a bigger context window will not fix your agent
item.description: Context rot is why long-running AI agents get worse as the prompt fills up. Why bigger windows do not help, and how I budget context to keep an agent sharp.

item.title: Why your real-time UI lies after a websocket reconnect
item.description: What happens to a real-time UI when the websocket drops and reconnects: stale data, missed events, and the resync patterns that keep live numbers honest.

item.title: Indexing on-chain data without losing your mind
item.description: Notes from building indexers for a top-10 crypto and a DeFi exchange. When The Graph is the right call, when Subsquid earns its keep, and the traps that cost me weekends.

item.title: Where AI coding agents earn their keep, and where they do not
item.description: An honest take after the hype: the tasks agents genuinely speed up, the ones where they quietly add risk, and how I would use them on a team.

item.title: How subgraphs actually work, from someone who ships them
item.description: A working engineer's guide to how The Graph subgraphs actually work, from event handlers and the store to reorg safety and the gotchas docs skip.

item.title: Handling chain reorgs without corrupting your indexer
item.description: How to handle chain reorgs in an indexer without corrupting state: confirmations, rollbacks, idempotent writes, and the quiet failure modes to fear.

item.title: Subsquid vs The Graph in 2026: a practical comparison
item.description: Subsquid vs The Graph in 2026 from someone who ships both: sync speed, query flexibility, hosting, and how to actually pick the right indexer.

item.title: What building a cross-chain bridge taught me about trust
item.description: Lessons from building a cross-chain bridge: where trust really lives, finality and replay risks, and why the boring parts are what keep funds safe.

item.title: The token swap frontend pitfalls that cost real money
item.description: Token swap frontend pitfalls that cost users real money: slippage, stale quotes, approvals, decimals, and the trust details most swap UIs get wrong.

item.title: Event ordering on EVM is not what you think
item.description: EVM event ordering trips up almost every indexer. Here is how logs are actually ordered on chain and the sort key you need to get it right.

item.title: Modeling on-chain data so your queries do not melt
item.description: On-chain data modeling decides whether your queries fly or crawl. Practical schema patterns for indexers that stay fast as the chain grows.

item.title: RPC reliability for Web3 apps that cannot go down
item.description: Web3 RPC reliability is where most production outages actually start. Patterns for failover, retries and fallbacks that keep your dapp up.

item.title: How a DeFi DEX is actually put together
item.description: A practical look at DeFi DEX architecture: the contracts, the indexer, the frontend, and where the genuinely hard engineering really lives.

item.title: Building a gas-aware frontend users trust
item.description: A gas-aware frontend tells users what a transaction will cost before they sign. Practical patterns for estimates, failures and honest UX.
